title: IAA's leadership team
body: "Heather Laing – Interim Chief Executive   Heather became the interim Chief Executive of the Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) in January 2025, bringing extensive experience from a diverse career spanning the public, private, and non-profit sectors.    An accomplished Senior Civil Servant, Heather has held several high-profile leadership roles in immigration. Before joining the Home Office in 2016, she built much of her career in fraud prevention, detection, and deterrence. Her work included positions in public sector and outsourced banking operations, as well as serving as a Non-Executive Director and Chair of the Advisory Board at Cifas, a leading fraud prevention agency.    As interim Chief Executive, Heather is focused on strengthening the IAA’s leadership team and driving the successful implementation of its transformation agenda. She plays a critical role in supporting the organisation’s evolution into a high-performing, outward-focused authority with enhanced capabilities, ensuring it delivers exceptional services to the public.    Annie Morris – Chief People Officer  Annie joined the Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) in December 2023 as Chief People Officer and Head of Communications, bringing over a decade of experience in communications, strategy, and governance across the United Nations and Civil Service.  Annie began her career in the humanitarian sector as a training lead for the UK’s UN Migration Agency, where she trained frontline professionals to support victims of trafficking and slavery. In 2015, she joined the Civil Service, starting at the Cabinet Office, where she took on various roles aligned with Prime Ministerial priorities. Notably, she led the communications response team following the Grenfell Tower tragedy.  Annie later worked at the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, where she supported communications in the lead-up to EU Exit. In 2019, she became Head of Communications and Engagement at the Investigatory Powers Commissioner’s Office.  At the IAA, Annie plays a critical leadership role, advising the Immigration Services Commissioner on strategic initiatives, communications, and engagement activities. She also serves as a liaison between the Executive Team and staff, fostering collaboration and ensuring effective communication across the organisation.  Karen Eyre-White – Director of Service Delivery  Karen joined the Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) in February 2023 as Director of Service Delivery, bringing a wealth of experience in policy, operational, and regulatory roles across government.    Karen began her Civil Service career in 2008 through the graduate Fast Stream, taking on policy and private office roles within the Department for Transport. Notably, she contributed to transport planning for the 2012 Olympic and Paralympic Games.    In 2013, Karen transitioned to the Home Office, where she led casework teams managing immigration judicial reviews. In 2015, she was appointed Chief Executive of the Sports Grounds Safety Authority, a government safety regulator and Arm’s Length Body of the Department for Culture, Media and Sport.    As Director of Service Delivery at the IAA, Karen oversees the regional teams, the authorisations team, and the intelligence team. Her role focuses on ensuring the effective regulation of immigration advisers and organisations, maintaining high-quality advice for those seeking support, and preventing the provision of unregulated advice.   Clare Hussain...
